author: IRENÆUS
book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus

ccel.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

title: XXXIII.

source.title: ANF01. The Apostolic Fathers with Justin Martyr and Irenaeus

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: Inasmuch as certain men, impelled by what considerations I know not, remove from God the half of His creative power, by asserting that He is merely the cause of quality resident in matter, and by maintaining that matter itself is uncreated, come now let us put the question, What is at any time … is immutable.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: But if matter be immutable, and the immutable suffers no change in regard to quality, it does not form the substance of the world.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: For which reason it seems to them superfluous, that God has annexed qualities to matter, since indeed matter admits of no possible alteration, it being in itself an uncreated thing.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: But further, if matter be uncreated, it has been made altogether according to a certain quality, and this immutable, so that it cannot be receptive of more qualities, nor can it be the thing of which the world is made.

verses.book: Fragments from the Lost Writings of Irenæus
verses.text: But if the world be not made from it, [this theory] entirely excludes God from exercising power on the creation [of the world].
